title: BAICALEIN
description: Baicalein is a bioactive flavonoid compound extracted from the roots of Scutellaria baicalensis (Chinese skullcap). It demonstrates neuroprotective, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties by modulating multiple cellular pathways including NLRP3 inflammasome, Nrf2/HO-1, and MAPK signaling cascades. Research indicates potential therapeutic applications in neurodegenerative diseases, anxiety, depression, and cognitive function through reduction of neuroinflammation and oxidative stress.

research.title: Mitigating neurodegenerative diseases: the protective influence of baicalin and baicalein through neuroinflammation regulation
research.summary: Baicalein and baicalin show protective effects in animal models of neurodegenerative diseases by inhibiting pro-inflammatory cytokines, suppressing microglial activation, and regulating inflammatory signaling cascades including TLR4, MAPK, and NLRP3 inflammasome pathways.

research.title: Neuroprotective effects of baicalin and baicalein on the central nervous system and the underlying mechanisms
research.summary: Multiple studies demonstrate that baicalein effectively reduces severity of depression, stroke, and CNS degenerative disorders by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, regulating programmed cell death, and reducing mitochondrial dysfunction.

research.title: The effects of baicalin in depression: preclinical evidence construction based on meta-analysis
research.summary: Meta-analysis of preclinical studies shows baicalin significantly improves depressive symptoms in animal models, increasing sucrose preference and reducing immobility in forced swim and tail suspension tests, demonstrating antidepressant potential.

research.title: Baicalein: Multiple Pharmacological Activities, Pharmacokinetics, and Clinical Trials
research.summary: Comprehensive review demonstrates baicalein's therapeutic potential across multiple conditions including neurodegenerative diseases and cardiovascular health through modulation of oxidative stress, inflammatory responses, and specific molecular pathways, showing it is well-tolerated as a potential nutritional supplement.

sentiment.summary: User experiences are limited in the provided data, but scientific literature demonstrates consistent neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects in preclinical models. The compound shows promise for mood, anxiety, and cognitive support, though human clinical data remains limited.

effects.label: Preclinical Evidence:
effects.description: Strong animal model data showing consistent benefits across depression, anxiety, and neuroprotection studies

effects.label: Mechanism Clarity:
effects.description: Well-documented molecular mechanisms including Nrf2/HO-1, NLRP3 inflammasome, and MAPK pathway modulation

effects.label: Bioavailability Considerations:
effects.description: Flavonoid compound with potential absorption limitations requiring appropriate formulation or delivery methods

effects.label: Clinical Translation:
effects.description: Promising preclinical results warrant human clinical trials to confirm therapeutic efficacy and optimal dosing

dosage.label: Research Doses:
dosage.description: Animal studies typically use doses ranging from 10-100 mg/kg body weight depending on condition

dosage.label: Human Equivalence:
dosage.description: Direct human equivalent doses need clinical validation but may range from moderate to higher supplemental doses

dosage.label: Timing:
dosage.description: Effects on neuroinflammation and neuroprotection suggest benefits from consistent daily administration

dosage.label: Formulation:
dosage.description: Bioavailability may be enhanced through specific delivery systems or combination with other compounds

sideEffects.label: General Tolerability:
sideEffects.description: Literature indicates baicalein is well-tolerated in preclinical studies with minimal adverse effects reported

sideEffects.label: Interaction Potential:
sideEffects.description: As a bioactive flavonoid, may interact with cytochrome P450 enzymes affecting drug metabolism

sideEffects.label: Limited Human Data:
sideEffects.description: Side effect profile in humans requires more clinical investigation

sideEffects.label: Dosing Considerations:
sideEffects.description: Higher doses may require monitoring though specific toxicity thresholds not well-established in humans

availability.label: Natural Source:
availability.description: Derived from Scutellaria baicalensis (Chinese skullcap), available as standardized extracts

availability.label: Supplement Market:
availability.description: Available through specialized supplement vendors focusing on nootropics and herbal compounds

availability.label: Research Status:
availability.description: Primarily studied in preclinical models with emerging interest for clinical application
